what_it_does: Configures a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server named company-channel that launches a local Node.js script with a COMPANY_ROOT environment variable.
observations: Declares a single MCP server under mcpServers.
Hard-codes absolute paths including a user home directory.
standouts: Absolute filesystem paths tied to a specific user home directory.
MCP server entry point is an .mjs ES module.
conventions: JSON configuration file for MCP server registry.
Uses command/args/env structure typical of MCP client configs.
concerns: Hard-coded absolute path /home/tim/company makes the config non-portable.
No documented description of what company-channel exposes.
notes: MCP client configuration wiring a local Node channel server to the company root directory.
questions: What capabilities or tools does company_channel.mjs expose?
Which MCP client consumes this config and where is it installed?
Should COMPANY_ROOT be configurable rather than hard-coded?
intention_signals: Provide an MCP client with a locally runnable company-channel server that operates within the /home/tim/company project tree.
contribution: Stores the launch recipe and environment binding for the company-channel MCP server, enabling an MCP client to start and contextualize the channel script.
summary_for_embedding: This file is an MCP server configuration that registers a local Node.js ES module channel server with a fixed COMPANY_ROOT environment variable.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to wire a local company-channel MCP server into an MCP client so it can run from the company project root.
